lines.en_lit: with regard to prohibition an unslaughtered animal carcass, they disagree.
Rabbi Akiva holds: It is a full-fledged prohibition, And Rabbi Yosei HaGelili holds: It is not a full-fledged prohibition;
Abaye said that everyone agrees that the prohibition of animal carcass is a full-fledged prohibition, and here, with regard to they disagree. you shall leave
Rabbi Akiva holds You shall leave, indicates from the outset, Rabbi Yosei HaGelili holds You shall leave, indicates now,

The Sages taught How does he confess? I have done wrong, I have rebelled, and I have sinned. And likewise, with regard to the scapegoat, it says “And he shall confess over it all of the children of Israel’s wrongdoings and all their rebellions and all their sins” And likewise, Moses it says: “Forgiving wrongdoing and rebellion and sin” the statement of Rabbi Meir. And the Rabbis say Wrongdoings are intentional and likewise it says: “That soul shall be cut off, it bears its guilt”

Rebellions are rebellious And likewise, it says: “The king of Moab rebelled [pasha] against me” And it is said: “Then Livna rebelled at that time” All of their sins, these are unwitting sins. And it says: “If a soul should sin unwittingly” once he confessed the wrongdoings and rebellions, he then confess the unwitting

Rather, this is he confesses: I have sinned, I have done wrong, and I have rebelled before You, I and my household. And likewise, with regard to David it says “We have sinned along with our forefathers, we have done wrong, we have performed evil” And likewise, with regard to Solomon it says: “We have sinned, and we have done wrong, we have done evil” And likewise, with regard to Daniel it says: “We have sinned, and we have done wrong, and we have done evil, and we have rebelled” However, what is which Moses said: Forgiving wrongdoing and rebellion and sin, Moses said before the Holy One, Blessed be He: Master of the Universe, when the Jewish people sin before you and repent, render their intentional sins like unwitting ones,

Rabba bar Shmuel said Rav said: halakha is in accordance with the statement of the Rabbis. obvious an individual and the many, halakha is in accordance with the many. Lest you say the rationale of Rabbi Meir is reasonable, as the verse Moses supports his teaches us

a certain who descended before Rabba, and he performed Rabbi Meir. said to him: Have you forsaken the Rabbis, and performed Rabbi Meir? said to Rabba: I hold in accordance with Rabbi Meir, as it is written in the Torah of Moses.
The Sages taught and atone the verse is speaking of atonement words you say atonement words, or perhaps it is only atonement blood,
I will infer Atonement is stated here, and atonement is stated there, Just as atonement that is stated with regard to the goat words, so too, atonement stated with regard to the bull words.
And if it is your wish to state It says: “And Aaron shall then offer his bull of sin-offering and atone for himself and his household. the bull has not yet been slaughtered.
What And if it is your wish to say, And if you say let us derive from the goat that is offered within, whose atonement is through blood it says: And atone, and the bull has not yet been slaughtered.
